tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

{"number":57359,"title":"ManagedIdentityCredential intermittently fails with socket permission error on App Service (169.254.169.254:80)","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Identity 1.13.1

### Describe the bug

ManagedIdentityCredential intermittently fails to acquire a token with a socket permission error when reaching IMDS (169.254.169.254:80). The same code works consistently on our dev app service but fails frequently on production. Both have System Assigned Managed Identity enabled and run the same workloads. Requests sometimes succeed on retry, but occasionally fail completely after all 6 retry attempts.   

### Expected behavior

Token acquisition should succeed reliably, or provide a more descriptive error when IMDS is rate limited.

### Actual behavior

  Exception:

Azure.Identity.CredentialUnavailableException: ManagedIdentityCredential authentication unavailable. Multiple attempts failed to obtain a token from the managed identity endpoint.
 ---> MSAL.NetCore.4.66.1.0.MsalServiceException:
	ErrorCode: managed_identity_request_failed
Microsoft.Identity.Client.MsalServiceException: Retry failed after 6 tries. Retry settings can be adjusted in ClientOptions.Retry or by configuring a custom retry policy in ClientOptions.RetryPolicy. (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80))
 ---> System.AggregateException: Retry failed after 6 tries. Retry settings can be adjusted in ClientOptions.Retry or by configuring a custom retry policy in ClientOptions.RetryPolicy. (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80))
 ---> Azure.RequestFailedException: An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)
 ---> System.Net.Http.HttpRequestException: An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)
 ---> System.Net.Sockets.SocketException (10013): An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions.
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs.ThrowException(SocketError error, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs.System.Threading.Tasks.Sources.IValueTaskSource.GetResult(Int16 token)
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.g__WaitForConnectWithCancellation|285_0(AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs saea, ValueTask connectTask, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at System.Net.Http.HttpConnectionPool.ConnectToTcpHostAsync(String host, Int32 port, HttpRequestMessage initialRequest, Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
                                                                                                                                                       

### Reproduction Steps

-  Azure App Service with System Assigned Managed Identity enabled
- A single Hangfire background job runs daily that creates a ManagedIdentityCredential
     to access Azure Key Vault via SecretClient

  Code:

  var credentials = new ManagedIdentityCredential(
      new ManagedIdentityCredentialOptions
      {
          Retry =
          {
              MaxRetries = 5,
              Delay = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(2),
              Mode = RetryMode.Exponential,
          }
      });

  var secretClient = new SecretClient(new Uri(keyVaultUrl), credentials);
  var secret = await secretClient.GetSecretAsync(secretName);

### Environment

{"issues":[{"number":57359,"title":"ManagedIdentityCredential intermittently fails with socket permission error on App Service (169.254.169.254:80)","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Identity 1.13.1

### Describe the bug

ManagedIdentityCredential intermittently fails to acquire a token with a socket permission error when reaching IMDS (169.254.169.254:80). The same code works consistently on our dev app service but fails frequently on production. Both have System Assigned Managed Identity enabled and run the same workloads. Requests sometimes succeed on retry, but occasionally fail completely after all 6 retry attempts.   

### Expected behavior

Token acquisition should succeed reliably, or provide a more descriptive error when IMDS is rate limited.

### Actual behavior

  Exception:

Azure.Identity.CredentialUnavailableException: ManagedIdentityCredential authentication unavailable. Multiple attempts failed to obtain a token from the managed identity endpoint.
 ---> MSAL.NetCore.4.66.1.0.MsalServiceException:
	ErrorCode: managed_identity_request_failed
Microsoft.Identity.Client.MsalServiceException: Retry failed after 6 tries. Retry settings can be adjusted in ClientOptions.Retry or by configuring a custom retry policy in ClientOptions.RetryPolicy. (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80))
 ---> System.AggregateException: Retry failed after 6 tries. Retry settings can be adjusted in ClientOptions.Retry or by configuring a custom retry policy in ClientOptions.RetryPolicy. (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)) (An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80))
 ---> Azure.RequestFailedException: An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)
 ---> System.Net.Http.HttpRequestException: An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions. (169.254.169.254:80)
 ---> System.Net.Sockets.SocketException (10013): An attempt was made to access a socket in a way forbidden by its access permissions.
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs.ThrowException(SocketError error, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs.System.Threading.Tasks.Sources.IValueTaskSource.GetResult(Int16 token)
   at System.Net.Sockets.Socket.g__WaitForConnectWithCancellation|285_0(AwaitableSocketAsyncEventArgs saea, ValueTask connectTask, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at System.Net.Http.HttpConnectionPool.ConnectToTcpHostAsync(String host, Int32 port, HttpRequestMessage initialRequest, Boolean async, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
                                                                                                                                                       

### Reproduction Steps

-  Azure App Service with System Assigned Managed Identity enabled
- A single Hangfire background job runs daily that creates a ManagedIdentityCredential
     to access Azure Key Vault via SecretClient

  Code:

  var credentials = new ManagedIdentityCredential(
      new ManagedIdentityCredentialOptions
      {
          Retry =
          {
              MaxRetries = 5,
              Delay = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(2),
              Mode = RetryMode.Exponential,
          }
      });

_No response_","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"agurevich25"},"created_at":"2026-03-23T15:04:05Z","updated_at":"2026-03-23T15:05:45Z"},{"number":57358,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.OpenAI - Adding Tools to ChatCompletionOptions disables content filtering","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.OpenAI 2.9.0-beta.1

### Describe the bug

When you send a request that should be filtered by content filter to `AzureOpenAIClient`, but you include any tools in the `ChatCompletionOptions` it doesn't get filtered. Just removing the tools and asking the same prompt makes filtering work again. 
An important note is that it didn't start failing after a library upgrade, but just randomly started failing around 18th or 19th of March 2026. At that time I was using `Azure.AI.OpenAI 2.2.0-beta.4`, but after upgrading to the latest version it still doesn't work.

### Expected behavior

`CompleteChatAsync` and `CompleteChatStreamingAsync` methods return `System.ClientModel.ClientResultException: HTTP 400 (content_filter)` when asked a question that should be filtered out.


### Actual behavior

LLM responds that it's sorry that it can't answer. However the filter doesn't trigger and an exception isn't thrown.

### Reproduction Steps

```cs
AzureOpenAIClient openAiClient = new AzureOpenAIClient(/*endpoint, credential*/);
ChatTool getTestTool = ChatTool.CreateFunctionTool(
    functionName: "test",
    functionDescription: "Test tool",
    functionParameters: BinaryData.FromObjectAsJson(new
        {
            Type = "object",
            Properties = new
            {

            },
            Required = new[] { "" },
        },
        new JsonSerializerOptions() { PropertyNamingPolicy = JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase }
    )
);

List messages =
[
    new UserChatMessage(/*A prompt that should be filtered here*/)
];

ChatCompletionOptions options = new()
{
    Tools = { getTestTool }
};

var deploymentName = "gpt-4o";
var chatClient = openAiClient.GetChatClient(deploymentName);
var response =  await chatClient.CompleteChatAsync(messages, options);
Console.WriteLine(response.Value.Content[0].Text);
```

In this case the prompt won't get filtered out even if user asks questions related to self harm or illegal activities. However if you just comment out the `Tools = { getTestTool }` from `ChatCompletionOptions` initialization it will filter correctly and throws `ClientResultException` as expected.

### Environment

I'm using gpt-4o model version 2024-11-20, but I checked it also on gpt-5.2 version 2025-12-11 and same thing happens","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"Mako217"},"labels":["Service Attention","Client","customer-reported","question","needs-team-attention","OpenAI"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-03-23T14:40:09Z","updated_at":"2026-03-23T14:45:50Z"},{"number":57342,"title":"[QUERY] New version of Azure.ResourceManager.DevTestLabs","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.ResourceManager.DevTestLabs 1.1.1

### Query/Question

The current version of Azure.ResourceManager.DevTestLabs has a bug when loading virtual machine auto shutdown config. It throws exception when webhook url not set.

Problem code in 1.1.1
```
if (item3.Value.ValueKind != JsonValueKind.Null)
{
	webHookUri = new Uri(item3.Value.GetString());
}
```

Latest code
```
if (prop.Value.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Null)
{
	continue;
}
webhookUri = string.IsNullOrEmpty(prop.Value.GetString()) ? null : new Uri(prop.Value.GetString(), UriKind.RelativeOrAbsolute);                    
```


I found this is already fixed in latest code, can you release a new version?

### Environment

_No response_","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"zhiweiv"},"labels":["Devtestlab","Mgmt","customer-reported","question","needs-team-attention"],"comments":2,"created_at":"2026-03-23T05:56:52Z","updated_at":"2026-03-23T06:18:05Z"},{"number":57334,"title":"[MPG Generator Bug] ModelFactory method for flattened properties passes `default` instead of constructing inner properties object","body":"## Bug Description

The new TypeSpec MPG generator produces a **broken ModelFactory method** for `PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData`. The factory method accepts `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, and `provisioningState` as parameters, but passes `default` (null) for the entire `PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties` object in the constructor call — **silently discarding all three values**. Anyone using this factory for mocking will get an empty object with all domain-specific properties as `null`.

## Affected Package

- **Package**: `Azure.ResourceManager.Purview`
- **TypeSpec spec**: `specification/purview/resource-manager/Microsoft.Purview/Purview` (commit `[REDACTED]` in Azure/azure-rest-api-specs)
- **Emitter**: `eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-mgmt-emitter-package.json`

## Generated Code (Buggy)

**File**: `src/Generated/ArmPurviewModelFactory.cs` (lines 394–412)

```csharp
public static PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData(
    ResourceIdentifier id = default,
    string name = default,
    ResourceType resourceType = default,
    SystemData systemData = default,
    ResourceIdentifier privateEndpointId = default,              // ← accepted
    PurviewPrivateLinkServiceConnectionState connectionState = default,  // ← accepted
    string provisioningState = default)                           // ← accepted
{
    return new PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData(
        id,
        name,
        resourceType,
        systemData,
        additionalBinaryDataProperties: null,
        default);   // ← BUG: passes default for PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties,
                     //   discarding privateEndpointId, connectionState, provisioningState
}
```

## Data Model Structure

The `PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData` resource uses a "properties envelope" pattern — the REST API returns:

```json
{
  "id": "...",
  "name": "...",
  "type": "...",
  "properties": {
    "privateEndpoint": { "id": "..." },
    "privateLinkServiceConnectionState": { ... },
    "provisioningState": "..."
  }
}
```

The generator correctly **flattens** the inner properties onto the C# class:

```csharp
// PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData.cs
internal PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties Properties { get; set; }

public ResourceIdentifier PrivateEndpointId     // delegates to Properties.PrivateEndpointId
public PurviewPrivateLinkServiceConnectionState ConnectionState  // delegates to Properties.ConnectionState
public string ProvisioningState                  // delegates to Properties.ProvisioningState
```

But the **ModelFactory fails to reconstruct** the `PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties` from the flattened parameters.

## Expected Behavior (Same Pattern Works for PurviewAccountData)

The generator correctly handles the **identical pattern** for `PurviewAccountData` — it wraps the flattened parameters into a `PurviewAccountProperties` object:

```csharp
// ArmPurviewModelFactory.cs — PurviewAccountData (CORRECT)
public static PurviewAccountData PurviewAccountData(
    ...,
    PurviewAccountStatus accountStatus = default,
    string cloudConnectorsAwsExternalId = default,
    ...)
{
    return new PurviewAccountData(
        ...,
        accountStatus is null && ... ? default : new PurviewAccountProperties(
            accountStatus,
            new CloudConnectors(cloudConnectorsAwsExternalId, null),
            ...),                        // ← correctly constructs inner properties object
        ...);
}
```

## Correct Fix (Applied as Customization Workaround)

```csharp
// ArmPurviewModelFactory.Compat.cs — manual override
public static PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData(
    ResourceIdentifier id = default,
    string name = default,
    ResourceType resourceType = default,
    SystemData systemData = default,
    ResourceIdentifier privateEndpointId = default,
    PurviewPrivateLinkServiceConnectionState connectionState = default,
    string provisioningState = default)
{
    return new PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData(
        id,
        name,
        resourceType,
        systemData,
        additionalBinaryDataProperties: null,
        privateEndpointId is null && connectionState is null && provisioningState is null
            ? default
            : new PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties(
                privateEndpointId is null ? default : new PrivateEndpoint(privateEndpointId, null),
                connectionState,
                provisioningState,
                null));
}
```

## Root Cause Analysis

The generator has logic to:
1. ✅ Flatten inner properties onto the resource data class (works correctly)
2. ✅ Accept flattened parameters in the ModelFactory method signature (works correctly)
3. ❌ Reconstruct the inner properties object from flattened parameters in the ModelFactory body (**fails for some resource types**)

The bug appears to be in the ModelFactory code generation logic in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt` — specifically, the code that handles reconstructing nested property objects when generating factory method bodies. It works for `PurviewAccountProperties` but fails for `PrivateEndpointConnectionProperties`.

## Impact

- **Severity**: Medium — affects mocking/testing scenarios
- **Scope**: Potentially affects any resource type with a flattened `properties` envelope where the generator doesn't reconstruct the inner object
- **Workaround**: Manual `ModelFactory` override in `Customization/` (applied in this PR)

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Generate `Azure.ResourceManager.Purview` SDK from the TypeSpec spec
2. Use the generated `ArmPurviewModelFactory.PurviewPrivateEndpointConnectionData()` method with non-default `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, or `provisioningState`
3. Observe that the returned object has all three properties as `null`/`default`","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"mzhongl524"},"labels":["bug"],"created_at":"2026-03-23T03:33:08Z","updated_at":"2026-03-23T03:33:08Z"},{"number":57325,"title":"TCGC 0.66.3 is causing issues with PlanetaryComputer.","body":"0.66.3 is causing issues with PlanetaryComputer.

arguments.body: Routing to the team for assistance: @JonathanCrd

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member best able to assist.

**Summary:** `ManagedIdentityCredential` (Azure.Identity 1.13.1) intermittently fails with a `SocketException (10013)` — "access permissions denied" — when connecting to the IMDS endpoint `169.254.169.254:80` on Azure App Service. The issue is intermittent, production-specific, and exhausts all 6 retry attempts.

<details>
<summary>🔍 Issue Analysis</summary>

**Root cause hypothesis:**  
`SocketException 10013` (`WSAEACCES`) typically means the OS/network stack rejected the connection at a socket level — not an IMDS rate limit. On Azure App Service, this can occur when:
- A firewall or NSG rule intermittently blocks outbound traffic to the link-local range `169.254.x.x`
- The App Service sandbox restricts raw socket access during high CPU/memory pressure
- A platform-side networking event (SNAT exhaustion, sandbox resource limits) temporarily blocks the connection

This differs from an IMDS rate-limit (HTTP 429), which would surface as an HTTP error rather than a socket-level rejection.

**Why production but not dev?**  
Production environments tend to have stricter firewall/NSG configurations, higher load (more SNAT connections), or tighter App Service plan resource limits.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Debugging Strategies</summary>

1. **Check App Service networking diagnostics:**  
   In the Azure Portal → App Service → Diagnose and solve problems → "Network checks" to look for SNAT exhaustion or connectivity issues to `169.254.169.254`.

2. **Validate IMDS reachability:**  
   Use the App Service Kudu console (Advanced Tools) to run:  
   ```
   curl -H "Metadata: true" "http://169.254.169.254/metadata/identity/oauth2/token?api-version=2018-02-01&resource=https://vault.azure.net/"
   ```

3. **Enable verbose Azure.Identity logging** to capture token request timelines:
   ```csharp
   AzureEventSourceListener.CreateConsoleLogger(EventLevel.Verbose);
   ```

4. **Reuse `ManagedIdentityCredential`:**  
   Create the credential once (e.g., as a singleton) rather than creating a new instance per Hangfire job invocation — this avoids repeated cold-start IMDS calls.

5. **Consider `DefaultAzureCredential` with `ManagedIdentityClientId`** as a fallback pattern.

</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Selection</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — This is a bug/behavior issue in the Azure.Identity client SDK (`Azure.Identity` package)
- **Service:** `Azure.Identity` — Explicitly stated in the issue (version 1.13.1); confirmed by multiple reference issues with the same label pair (e.g., #56525, #52122, #50268)
- **Confidence:** High — the package name is explicitly stated, the category is unambiguous (client SDK, not management or provisioning)

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔗 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [Azure.Identity Managed Identity Credential docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.identity.managedidentitycredential)
- [Troubleshoot managed identity on App Service](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/app-service/overview-managed-identity)
- [IMDS endpoint documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/virtual-machines/instance-metadata-service)
- [Azure SDK retry documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/azure-sdk-for-dotnet-threadpool-and-async)

</details>
